reasoning: The base rate for a newly developed vaccine progressing to approval and subsequent inclusion on a Wikipedia "List of infectious diseases" page within a 6-month time frame is extremely low. Current medical literature as of mid-2025 indicates that treatments and vaccines for HTLV-1 are still in theoretical or pre-clinical animal study stages (e.g., RNA-based therapeutics), and no vaccine has been approved. Precedent from other high-profile diseases on the same Wikipedia list (like HIV or Hepatitis C, which have experimental candidates but no approved vaccine) confirms that only fully developed and approved vaccines get listed. Given the slow nature of clinical trials and the lack of an ongoing Phase III trial close to successful completion for HTLV-1, it is highly likely that no vaccine will be listed by any of the assessment dates. I've maintained probabilities at the lowest allowed threshold (0.05) or slightly above, to reflect standard epistemic uncertainty scaling with the forecast horizon.
